purpose: Shared coordination state for Codex, Claude and future agents working on the a2waspwarfare developer wiki.

Codex.role: Integrator, wiki UX owner, source-atlas maintainer, publisher and validation runner.

Claude.role: Autonomous independent reviewer, contradiction hunter, subsystem archaeologist and implementation-readiness reviewer.

Hilbert.role: Read-only Codex sub-agent for network/PV boundary archaeology.

Cicero.role: Read-only Codex sub-agent for server gameplay loops, towns, economy and AI commander systems.

Curie.role: Read-only Codex sub-agent for UI/HUD/dialogs/client UX archaeology.

Meitner.role: Read-only Codex sub-agent for tooling, generated missions, extension, DiscordBot, BattlEye and server integration archaeology.

activeClaims.handoff: Codex and Claude should update the dashboard/status snapshot whenever visible lane ownership changes.
Claude and Hilbert should review purchase authority boundaries and the missing RequestBuyUnit PVF; Cicero should review whether AIBuyUnit has any dynamic AI commander caller.
Future implementation should validate high-impact registered PVF handlers after the dispatch hardening fix.
Future implementation should review commander reassignment, AI commander runtime ownership, supply mission authority and hosted FPS loops.
Future UI work should resolve stale RscMenu_Upgrade, duplicate IDDs and suspect RscClickableText soundPush before adding new dialogs.
Future tooling work should use a correctly named a2waspwarfare checkout before running LoadoutManager.
Claude should read Agent-Collaboration-Protocol.md and append claim/finding/complete events for future review passes.
Claude should review construction authority boundaries, server-side validation and generated-mission propagation hazards after the atlas lands.
Codex should integrate confirmed findings into owning atlas pages after current coordination pass.

Code owners: apply the behavior-preserving dispatch validation (resolve SRVFNC/CLTFNC via missionNamespace getVariable instead of Call Compile) in Chernarus source and add a real BattlEye publicvariable.txt; then run LoadoutManager. Codex: optionally link the playbook from Networking/SQF atlas.

Code owners: add player to construction request payloads and validate side+commander+funds (and base-area avail) server-side before creating objects; pair with DR-1. Codex: this deepens the Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas Authority Boundary section — optionally cross-link DR-6 there.

Code owners: defensive shape-validate the callExtension return before use (no parseSimpleArray in A2 OA), add a DB circuit-breaker, chunk SEND_PLAYERLIST, auto-detect the external DLL. Codex: document the A2WaspDatabase external dependency + call-compile trust contract on External-Integrations (its lane).

lanes.summary: Human and machine-readable progress dashboard for Codex/Claude lane tracking.
Deep-read client buy menus, factory queues, unit config, server AI buying and purchase authority boundaries.
Sub-agent deep-index of publicVariable/PVF registration, authority boundaries and BattlEye filter risks.
Sub-agent deep-index of towns, economy, commander, AI teams, supply/value loops and server performance.
Sub-agent deep-index of Rsc resources, HUD/RHUD, dialogs, menus, gear templates and client UX state.
Sub-agent deep-index of tools, generated missions, extensions, DiscordBot, BattlEye and server configs.
Deep-read construction, CoIn, HQ deployment, repair, sale and server creation flows.
Claude may self-select the next bounded, source-backed subsystem or risk review without waiting for Codex assignment.
Claude published a behavior-preserving PVF dispatch hardening playbook; code owners should review before implementation.
Move Claude deep-review findings into Feature-Status-Register, Networking and Tools pages.

rules: Update Progress-Dashboard.md and agent-status.json when visible ownership or lane state changes.
Append, do not overwrite, Agent-Worklog.md and agent-events.jsonl.
Use source-cited evidence for broken/partial-feature claims.
If a branch is stale, integrate targeted findings instead of merging over newer navigation.
Keep docs/wiki and the GitHub wiki checkout in parity before publishing.
Use Arma 2 OA scripting references only.
